summary: postinstall silently installs an unpinned, unrelated global npm package from a third-party mirror
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall script (dist/postinstall.js) spawns a detached, output-suppressed `npm install -g opencode-ai --registry=https://registry.npmmirror.com`. The dependency is unpinned (`@latest`), pulled from a third-party mirror rather than the default registry, has a different publisher, and is unrelated to the package's advertised IM/messenger bridge purpose; the install runs detached with stdio ignored so failures are invisible. Whoever controls `opencode-ai@latest` on that mirror obtains arbitrary code execution at global scope on every installer. Additionally, the same postinstall path invokes `setupService()` (dist/service-installer.js, dist/github-downloader.js, dist/daemon-manager.js) which globally installs an unpinned `claw-subagent-service@latest` and registers it as an elevated system service via PowerShell `Start-Process -Verb RunAs` on Windows, `pkexec`/`sudo` on Linux, and `osascript... with administrator privileges` on macOS — giving the author the ability to push new code that auto-starts as SYSTEM/root on every boot without version review. The same flow runs `forceUninstallService()`, which kills processes with overbroad patterns (`pkill -9 -f daemon.js`, `pkill -9 -f worker.js`, `taskkill /f /im opencode.exe`, and a WMIC delete for any `node.exe` whose commandline contains `claw-subagent`), terminating unrelated developer processes on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
